evals.prompt: I want a loop that automatically fixes failing tests in my repo every time CI fails. The repo uses TypeScript and Jest. Tests run on GitHub Actions. When a test fails I currently have to manually read the error, edit the code, push, and wait. I want that whole cycle to happen automatically.
evals.expected_output: 6 separate labeled files: goal-card.md, architecture.md, cycle.md, building-blocks.md, implementation.md, reconciliation-template.md. Goal Card is a standalone artifact. Architecture explicitly covers both single/fleet AND closed/open dimensions. Cycle maps to real GitHub Actions commands. Implementation has runnable content.
assertions: Output organized into distinct labeled phase files: goal-card.md, architecture.md, cycle.md, building-blocks.md, implementation.md, reconciliation-template.md — NOT a single monolithic document
goal-card.md is a standalone artifact presented before architecture design begins (not merged into another file)
architecture.md explicitly addresses BOTH dimensions: single-agent vs fleet (with rationale) AND closed vs open loop (with rationale)
reconciliation-template.md present as a separate file containing all required fields: Trigger, Goal, Cycle, Discover, Plan, Execute, Verify, Iterate, Outcome, Memory, Next trigger
cycle.md maps stages to THIS task concretely — e.g. Discover specifies reading GitHub Actions logs by name, not just 'find what is needed'
implementation.md contains actual runnable commands (real gh CLI flags, real workflow YAML, or real shell script content) — not described in prose

evals.prompt: Design me a loop that researches a topic I give it, finds credible sources, verifies the claims, and saves a final synthesized report as a markdown file. It should keep searching until it's confident the answer is well-sourced, not just give me the first thing it finds.
evals.expected_output: 6 separate labeled files. Goal Card has confidence threshold as DONE WHEN. Architecture recommends single-agent. Cycle has separate verify agent. implementation.md is a usable SKILL.md.
assertions: Output organized into distinct labeled phase files: goal-card.md, architecture.md, cycle.md, building-blocks.md, implementation.md, reconciliation-template.md — NOT a single monolithic document
goal-card.md is a standalone artifact with DONE WHEN expressed as a confidence threshold or verified-source count (not vague 'when satisfied')
architecture.md explicitly addresses BOTH dimensions: single-agent vs fleet (with rationale) AND closed vs open loop (with rationale)
reconciliation-template.md present as a separate file containing all required fields: Trigger, Goal, Cycle, Discover, Plan, Execute, Verify, Iterate, Outcome, Memory, Next trigger
cycle.md specifies a SEPARATE verify/critic agent from the researcher — maker and checker are distinct (not same agent checking own work)
implementation.md contains actual runnable content with specific file paths for where reports are saved and real commands for searching/reading sources

evals.prompt: Every morning I manually prompt Claude to review yesterday's merged PRs in my GitHub repo and post a Slack summary with highlights and anything that looks risky. I want to automate this so it runs on its own at 9am without me doing anything. The repo is octocat/hello-world and Slack channel is #eng-updates.
evals.expected_output: 6 separate labeled files. Goal Card has trigger=9am and done-when=Slack posted. Architecture covers both dimensions. building-blocks.md has concrete automation config (launchd/cron). implementation.md has real gh CLI and Slack commands.
assertions: Output organized into distinct labeled phase files: goal-card.md, architecture.md, cycle.md, building-blocks.md, implementation.md, reconciliation-template.md — NOT a single monolithic document
goal-card.md is a standalone artifact with TRIGGER specifying daily 9am schedule and DONE WHEN specifying Slack message posted
architecture.md explicitly addresses BOTH dimensions: single-agent vs fleet (with rationale) AND closed vs open loop (with rationale)
reconciliation-template.md present as a separate file containing all required fields: Trigger, Goal, Cycle, Discover, Plan, Execute, Verify, Iterate, Outcome, Memory, Next trigger
building-blocks.md Automations row contains actual automation config (launchd plist XML snippet, cron entry, or GitHub Actions YAML) — not just 'use launchd'
implementation.md contains real gh CLI command for fetching merged PRs (with actual flags like --repo, --json, --state) AND real Slack posting mechanism (webhook curl or MCP tool call)

substance_assertions._note: Filename-INDEPENDENT loop-design QUALITY checks (added 2026-06-24 after iter-4 showed the 6 structural assertions measured FORMAT conformance: structural delta +100pp vs substance delta only +16.5pp). Grade these against output SUBSTANCE regardless of filenames, to separate quality from format. Run alongside the per-eval structural assertions.
assertions: Specifies a concrete STOP / success exit criterion (when the loop is DONE) anywhere in the output — concrete, not 'when satisfied'
Specifies an ESCAPE hatch: max failed iterations and/or escalation to human, anywhere
Verify is done by a SEPARATE checker from the executor (maker != checker), anywhere
Contains actually-runnable commands (real CLI flags / YAML / shell / API), not just prose, anywhere
States an OBSERVABLE success criterion (threshold/count/event), not subjective, anywhere
Addresses BOTH single-agent-vs-fleet AND closed-vs-open dimensions with rationale, anywhere

metadata.iter4_finding: structural pass delta = +100pp (format), substance delta = +16.5pp (true quality). Skill's value is mostly consistent structure+gates; modest substance uplift.
